forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure extends from a high south of Perth, across waters well south of SA. The high slowly moves eastwards to be centred well south of the Bight later and over the Tasman Sea by Saturday afternoon, with further high pressure systems building well south of the Bight. A trough over inland WA is expected to move across western and northern SA on Sunday and into the new week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Possible isolated showers in the far west. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures near to above average in the northwest but below average elsewhere. East to southeast winds, fresh about the north.
Possible showers and the chance of a thunderstorm in the far west. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures above average. East to northeasterly winds, with local sea breezes.

Showers, mainly about western districts. Chance of a thunderstorm in the far northwest. Maximum temperatures above average, tending near to below average in the north and southwest. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Showers, tending to rain about the north with locally heavy falls possible. The chance of a thunderstorm in the north. Maximum temperature mostly below average, near average in the far east. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: Generally less than 5mm, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the Pastoral districts, reaching 20 to 40 mm about the north. Isolated falls of 5 to 15 mm are possible about parts of the agricultural area and the southwest, 10-30 mm about the Pastoral districts and isolated falls of 40 to 80 mm are possible about the far north.
